items.title: 'Evil inside him': Dezi Freeman's nephew speaks out on cop killer uncle's death

children.content: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.

articles.title: What we know about where Dezi Freeman made his last stand

children.content: The rural property where police finally caught up with the fugitive gunman was a good place to disappear.

image.altText: A black police armourned vehicle is parked next to a shipping container. A black pipe is connected between the two.

articles.title: Freeman death stirs shock, sadness, anger in scarred Porepunkah

children.content: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

items.title: 'It's going to be painful every year': Zomi Frankcom's family call for answers

image.altText: Image of Zomi Frankcom smiling while wearing a cap and a black t-shirt with the WCK logo. Behind her are a lot of kids.

children.content: Two years ago on Wednesday, 1 April, Australian aid worker Zomi Frankcom was killed by an IDF strike in Gaza. Her family still want answers and accountability.

items.title: RBA to remove surcharges on debit, credit cards and card networks

children.content: The removal of surcharges on debit and credit cards is one of the sweeping reforms from the Reserve Bank.

items.title: Disability agency adds nearly 2,000 new jobs as it seeks to rein in costs

children.content: The agency that runs the National Disability Insurance Scheme defends hiring nearly 2,000 new employees last year despite major changes being rolled out to rein in the program's cost.

image.altText: The exterior of a building with NDIS signage prominently displayed on the outside. Location is not specific.

articles.title: A minute before Trump's wild Iran claim, oil traders made 'abnormal' moves

children.content: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

articles.title: How to tell this missile image is real

children.content: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

items.title: Man accused of failed Invasion Day rally bomb may enter insanity plea

items.title: Socceroos smash Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell

children.content: The Socceroos have racked up quick second half goals to beat Curaçao 5-1 in their World Cup farewell match in Melbourne.

items.title: Australia has not yet reached 'tipping point' for WFH directive, says expert

children.content: The fuel crisis is putting pressure on workers who rely on car travel and those who meet with vulnerable clients, with fresh calls for frontline support staff to be exempt from any future fuel rationing mandates.

articles.title: Early government modelling shows how long fuel stockpile lasts if imports slow

children.content: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

children.content: Thousands of Chinese social media users have started using images of US celebrity Kris Jenner as their profile photos in hopes it will bring them success and good fortune.

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adults

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds
